info.title: Pricing Pipeline API
info.description:
Multi-partner pricing ingestion + harmonisation + Data Quality + anomaly detection.

**Path A — POST /pipeline**: one-click 9-step orchestrator.
**Path B — sub-modules**: /load-data, /compute-dq, /detect-anomalies, /harmonise-product. Independently callable; combine in sequence to cover the same 9 steps with degraded gating semantics.

get.description: Standard liveness probe for container-orchestration platforms.

get.summary: DB-wide aggregate stats for the live visualization dashboard
get.description: Cross-batch totals + harmonise distribution + per-rule pass rates + pipeline funnel + recent bad-record samples. Reads across ALL batches currently in the database — not scoped to a single source_batch_id. Powers `submission/pipeline_runner.html` and refreshes whenever the frontend polls this endpoint.

post.summary: One-click orchestrator — runs all 9 steps end-to-end
post.description:
Runs the canonical 9-step pipeline in interleaved order:

  1. Parse CSV → `stg_price_offer`
  2. INGEST-stage Data Quality
  3. Harmonise raw product names
  4. PRE_FACT-stage Data Quality (HIGH-severity gate)
  5. Insert into `fact_price_offer` + payment child (**only PRE_FACT-passing rows**)
  6. SEMANTIC-stage Data Quality (soft signals; flag-and-keep)
  7. Update Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2 history
  8. Detect pricing anomalies on the new batch
  9. Write per-batch summary to `dws_partner_dq_per_batch`

Single transaction. PRE_FACT bad rows are hard-blocked from `fact_price_offer`. Equivalent to calling /load-data + /compute-dq + /detect-anomalies in sequence except for stronger gating semantics (the sub-module path lets bad rows enter fact and flags them post-hoc).

post.summary: Load CSV into the table (parse + harmonise + write fact + Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2)
post.description:
Task B sub-module — covers pipeline steps **1, 3, 5, 7, 9**:

  1. Parse CSV into `stg_price_offer`
  3. Harmonise raw product names against the canonical registry
  5. Insert change events into `fact_price_offer` + payment child (**no PRE_FACT gate** — all rows with parseable fields enter fact; Data Quality flagging happens post-hoc via /compute-dq)
  7. Update Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2 history
  9. Write batch summary

Steps 2/4/6 (Data Quality) and step 8 (anomaly detection) are NOT run here — call /compute-dq and /detect-anomalies separately, or use POST /pipeline for end-to-end with hard gating.

get.summary: Poll the progress / status of a load job

post.summary: Validate Data Quality — runs all 13 rules and writes to dq_output + dq_bad_records
post.description:
Task B sub-module — covers pipeline steps **2, 4, 6, 9**:

  2. INGEST-stage Data Quality (parse / format / required-field rules)
  4. PRE_FACT-stage Data Quality (country↔currency, partner↔country, harmonise match)
  6. SEMANTIC-stage Data Quality (soft signals — low-confidence harmonise, category sanity bounds)
  9. Refresh batch summary

All 13 rules execute against the batch. Per-rule pass rates are stored in **`dq_output`**; per-row violations (with the original CSV payload preserved as JSONB) are stored in **`dq_bad_records`**.

Designed to be called AFTER /load-data has populated fact_price_offer. PRE_FACT-failing rows are flagged post-hoc — they remain in fact and must be filtered via `LEFT JOIN dq_bad_records` if downstream queries need a clean view. For end-to-end execution with hard gating, use POST /pipeline instead.

post.summary: Detect pricing anomalies + return visualization payload
post.description:
Task B sub-module — covers pipeline step **8** (and refreshes step 9 summary).

For each offer in scope, compares its USD price against the 30-day rolling mean from `fact_partner_price_history` (the Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2 table). Returns the anomaly list with severity classification (HIGH ≥25%, MEDIUM ≥15%, LOW ≥10%) and a structured visualization payload (time series + baseline band + cross-partner comparison) that the frontend can render directly via Chart.js / Recharts.

get.summary: Harmonise a raw product name to canonical model(s) — Top-K + score
get.description:
Task B sub-module — exposes the harmoniser as an ad-hoc lookup. Returns up to **k** ranked candidate matches for a partner-supplied product name. Each candidate has a 0-1 score, a confidence bucket (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / MANUAL) and a per-signal breakdown for explainability.

Internally, pipeline step 3 (`harmonise_in_stg`) calls this same harmoniser for every staged row during /load-data and /pipeline.

get.summary: List records flagged by Data Quality rules
get.description: Powers the business-user review UI. Default filter: `status=NEW` to surface unresolved items. Pagination via `page` / `page_size`.

post.summary: Mark a bad record as resolved (or ignored)
post.description: Triggers the business-correction loop. When `replay_batch=true`, the associated `source_batch_id` is re-ingested with the updated abbreviation dictionary / rules — only that batch is re-processed.

is_real_anomaly.title: Is Real Anomaly
is_real_anomaly.description: True if sample is a real triggered anomaly; False if showing baseline-only illustration

AnomalyDashboardStats.title: AnomalyDashboardStats
AnomalyDashboardStats.description:
Anomaly detection summary across recent batches + one illustrative
visualization. When no real anomalies exist, the sample falls back to a
stable-baseline chart from the product with the richest history (still
real data, just no triggered anomaly point).

AnomalyType.title: AnomalyType
AnomalyType.description:
Designed signal taxonomy. Current implementation only emits `STATISTICAL`
(compares observed price against the 30-day rolling mean from
`fact_partner_price_history`). The other three are scoped as future work;
`fact_anomaly` already has columns to hold their results.

AnomalyVisualization.title: AnomalyVisualization
AnomalyVisualization.description: Structured payload for frontend chart libraries (Chart.js / Recharts).

BaselineSnapshot.title: BaselineSnapshot
BaselineSnapshot.description: Frozen snapshot of dws_product_price_baseline_1d at detection time.

source_batch_id.title: Source Batch Id
source_batch_id.description: Run all 13 DQ rules against this ingestion batch

DQRulePassRate.title: DQRulePassRate
DQRulePassRate.description: One rule's aggregated pass rate across all batches in the database.

DQRuleRunResult.title: DQRuleRunResult
DQRuleRunResult.description: One rule's run statistics — matches dq_output schema.

prefact_blocked_records.title: Prefact Blocked Records
prefact_blocked_records.description: Full distinct list of rows blocked by the PRE_FACT gate (1 row per offending stg row, even if it failed multiple PRE_FACT rules)

low_confidence_harmonise_records.title: Low Confidence Harmonise Records
low_confidence_harmonise_records.description: Full list of DQ_HARM_001 (low-confidence harmonise) records — flagged-and-kept under SEMANTIC policy

harmonise_samples.title: Harmonise Samples
harmonise_samples.description: Stratified harmonise examples with signal breakdown for the showcase 'Real Match Examples' card

DashboardStats.title: DashboardStats
DashboardStats.description:
DB-wide aggregate snapshot powering the live visualization dashboard.

Reads across ALL batches currently in the database — not scoped to a
single source_batch_id. Refreshes whenever the frontend polls the
GET /dashboard-stats endpoint.

rows_blocked_by_prefact.title: Rows Blocked By Prefact
rows_blocked_by_prefact.description: Distinct rows blocked at the PRE_FACT gate (across all batches)

harmonise_high_pct.title: Harmonise High Pct
harmonise_high_pct.description: Share of fact rows with HIGH harmonise confidence

total_violations.title: Total Violations
total_violations.description: Same as rows_bad — kept for headline-card naming clarity

date_range.description: Alternative: detect over a date range

after_harmonise.title: After Harmonise
after_harmonise.description: stg rows with product_model_id NOT NULL

sku_ids.title: Sku Ids
sku_ids.description: Original Product Ref IDs grouped under this model (color variants)

matches.title: Matches
matches.description: Top-K candidate matches, ranked descending by score

note.title: Note
note.description: Why this example is interesting (e.g. 'structural override fired')

HarmoniseSample.title: HarmoniseSample
HarmoniseSample.description:
One representative harmonise example with the full 3-signal breakdown,
designed for the showcase 'Real Match Examples' card on the dashboard.

chunk_count.title: Chunk Count
chunk_count.description: Total chunks the file was split into

rows_stg.title: Rows Stg
rows_stg.description: Rows in stg_price_offer for this batch (= rows parsed)

rows_loaded.title: Rows Loaded
rows_loaded.description: Rows in fact_price_offer for this batch

rows_unchanged.title: Rows Unchanged
rows_unchanged.description: Rows neither loaded to fact nor flagged bad

rows_bad.title: Rows Bad
rows_bad.description: Rows in dq_bad_records for this batch

dq_by_stage.title: Dq By Stage
dq_by_stage.description: DQ failed-record counts per stage: INGEST / PRE_FACT / SEMANTIC

PipelineResponse.title: PipelineResponse
PipelineResponse.description:
Aggregated result of the 9-step orchestrator.

The orchestrator interleaves the 4 sub-modules' work in the canonical
pipeline order (parse → ingest_dq → harmonise → prefact_dq → fact_write
with hard gate → semantic_dq → scd2 → anomaly_detection → summary).
Sub-module endpoints can replicate steps 1-9 in grouped fashion (see
/load-data, /compute-dq, /detect-anomalies); the orchestrator exists
for a one-shot call with strict PRE_FACT gating semantics.

replay_batch.title: Replay Batch
replay_batch.description: Re-ingest the batch with corrected rules / dictionary after resolving
